entry.title: 1. Transition Probability Matrix and value iteration
entry.content:
1. What is Markov chains and transition probability matrix?

1.1 Markov chains

In machine learning algorithms, Markov chains are widely applied in time series models. The main idea is that regardless of the initial state, as long as the state transition matrix remains unchanged, the final state will always converge to a fixed value. This memory-lessness is called the Markov property. The equation is as below:
$$
P(x_{t+1}∣…,x_{t−2},x_{t−1},x_{t})=P(x_{t+1}∣x_{t})\tag{1}
$$

1.2 Transition probability matrix

Each element of the matrix is represented by a probability. The values are non-negative, and the sum of the elements in each row equals 1. Under certain conditions, they can transition between each other, hence it is called a transition probability matrix. The two-step transition probability matrix is exactly the square of the one-step transition probability matrix. The 𝑘 step transition probability matrix is the 𝑘th power of the one-step transition probability matrix. In the 𝑘 step transition probability matrix, the sum of the elements in each row is also 1.

For example, the value of $$P(i,j)$$ in matrix is $$P(j|i)$$, which is the probability of from state $$i$$ to state $$j$$. The transition probability matrix is as below：
$$
\left[ \begin{matrix} 0.9 & 0.075 & 0.025 \ 0.15 & 0.8 & 0.05 \ 0.25 & 0.25 & 0.5 \end{matrix} \right] \tag{2}
$$

1.3 How Markov chain generates?

Giving an initial state $$P_{01}=[0.5, 0.2, 0.3]$$ and $$P_{02}=[0.1,0.4,0.5]$$, the 𝑘 step transition is $$P_0=P_0*P_k$$. $$P_0$$ can reach a stable value after multiple iterations.

If $$k=30$$, please calculate the eventual result.

1.3.1 Code and figure

Plot of iteration

1.3.2 Value of $$P_{01}$$ in each transition

Iteration$$i=1$$$$i=2$$$$i=3$$
1[0.5550.27250.1725]
2[0.58350.302750.11375]
3[0.5990.31440.0866]
4[0.607910.3180950.073995]
5[0.6133320.3185680.0681]
6[0.6168090.31787930.0653117]
7[0.619137920.316892040.06397004]
8[0.620750440.315941490.06330807]
9[0.621893640.315136490.06296987]
10[0.622717220.314493680.0627891]
11[0.623316820.313996010.06268716]
12[0.623756330.313617360.0626263]
13[0.624079880.313332190.06258793]
14[0.62431870.313118730.06256257]
15[0.624495280.312959530.06254519]
16[0.624625980.312841060.06253295]
17[0.624722780.312753040.06252418]
18[0.62479450.312687680.06251781]
19[0.624847660.312639190.06251315]
20[0.624887060.312603210.06250973]
21[0.624916270.312576530.0625072]
22[0.624937920.312556750.06250533]
23[0.624953970.312542070.06250395]
24[0.624965880.31253120.06250293]
25[0.62497470.312523130.06250217]
26[0.624981240.312517150.06250161]
27[0.624986090.312512710.06250119]
28[0.624989690.312509430.06250088]
29[0.624992350.312506990.06250066]
30[0.624994330.312505180.06250049]

1.3.3 Value of $$P_{02}$$ in each transition

Iteration$$i=1$$$$i=2$$$$i=3$$
1[0.2750.45250.2725]
2[0.38350.450750.16575]
3[0.45420.43080.115]
4[0.502150.4074550.090395]
5[0.5356520.3862240.078124]
6[0.55955140.36868410.0717645]
7[0.576840.354854760.06830524]
8[0.589460520.344223120.06631636]
9[0.598727030.336167120.06510585]
10[0.605555860.330114690.06432946]
11[0.610599840.32559080.06380936]
12[0.614330810.322219970.06344922]
13[0.617093030.319713090.06319388]
14[0.619139160.317850920.06300992]
15[0.620655360.316468650.06287598]
16[0.621779120.315443070.06277781]
17[0.622612120.314682340.06270554]
18[0.623229640.314118170.06265219]
19[0.623687450.31369980.06261274]
20[0.624026860.313389590.06258355]
21[0.62427850.313159570.06256193]
22[0.624465070.312989030.0625459]
23[0.624603390.312862580.06253403]
24[0.624705950.312768820.06252523]
25[0.624781980.312699310.0625187]
26[0.624838360.312647770.06251387]
27[0.624880160.312609560.06251028]
28[0.624911140.312581230.06250762]
29[0.624934120.312560230.06250565]
30[0.624951160.312544650.06250419]

$${P_{01}}^{30}=[0.62499433, 0.31250518, 0.06250049]$$, $${P_{02}}^{30}=[0.62495116 0.31254465 0.06250419]$$. From the tables and figure we can reach the conclusion that both states converge eventually and their value are close to each other.

2. Value iteration: a model-based algo

Unlike simply times initial state with transition probability matrix multiple times, value iteration is a commonly used dynamic planning (DP) method (DP methods assume that we have a perfect model of the environment’s MDP. That’s usually not the case in practice, but it’s important to study DP anyway), which is mainly used to solve the optimal strategy problem in Markov Decision Process (MDP). The core idea of the Value Iteration algorithm is to update the value function of the state iteratively, gradually approaching the optimal value function, so as to obtain the optimal policy. The main advantage of the value iteration algorithm is that it is simple and easy to implement, and it is applicable to various types of MDP problems. However, the main disadvantage of the value iteration algorithm is its high time complexity, especially when the state space is large. Therefore, in practical applications, the value iteration algorithm usually needs to be combined with other optimization techniques, such as dynamic programming optimization and parallel computing, to improve the computational efficiency. The equation of algorithm is as below.

2.1 Overview

$$
V^{(k+1)}(s) = max_a [R(s, a) + γ Σ_{s’ ∈ S} P(s’ | s, a) V^k(s’)]\tag{3}
$$

First, we start with a random value function $$V(s)$$. At each step, we update it. Hence, we look ahead one step and go over all possible actions at each iteration to find the maximum. Moreover, the only difference is that in the value iteration algorithm, we take the maximum number of possible actions. Instead of evaluating and then improving, the value iteration algorithm updates the state value function in a single step. In particular, this is possible by calculating all possible rewards by looking ahead. Finally, the value iteration algorithm is guaranteed to converge to the optimal values.

2.2 Implementation

This is the entire code of value iteration implementation. I will break down every line and explain.

It will be much easier to understand the algorithm with a practice. So we can try to explain value iteration well by a simple implementation. We first import NumPy, of course. Then definite a function, which is the main character of this section: value_iteration. This function requires several input values including states, actions, transition probability, reward, discount factor and theta. In this implementation, $$γ$$ = 0.9, $$θ$$ = $$1^{-6}$$.

In this function, we need to create two arrays for the storage of possible state and policy. State is easy to understand, but what is “policy”?

A policy is a strategy that an agent uses in pursuit of goals. The policy dictates the actions that the agent takes as a function of the agent’s state and the environment. In reinforcement learning, a policy is a strategy used by an agent to determine its actions at any given state. Formally, a policy is a mapping from states of the environment to actions to be taken when in those states. It can be deterministic or stochastic.

Deterministic Policy: This type of policy maps each state to a specific action. If $$π$$ is a deterministic policy and $$s$$ is a state, then $$π(s)$$ is the action taken when in state $$s$$.

Stochastic Policy: This type of policy provides a probability distribution over actions for each state. If $$π$$ is a stochastic policy and $$s$$ is a state, then $$π(a|s)$$ represents the probability of taking action $$a$$ when in state $$s$$.

Then we enter the iteration of value function. Before start iteration, we have to define a variable called Δ. It is a variable used to record the variation of value function in a iteration and can judge whether the iteration is converging. for s in states is a order asking agent to go through every state, v = value_function[s] is to store all the result of $$s$$ in $$v$$. These are the pre-moves of iteration.

The result of value function comes from the maximum value of action values. We create an empty array for the storage of action values. Firstly initialize action value, then for each action, we go through every possible next state. After cumulating action value based on bellman function and store them as an array, we can obtain the maximum action value and take it as the value function value of current state.

In order to judge the convergency, update delta (Δ) at the end of value function calculation. abs(v - value_function[s]) is the variation of value function. We determine the value of delta by comparing the value between previous Δ and new Δ and taking the maximum value. Cycle will ceased if the update range smaller than θ.

Simultaneously, we try to find the optimal policy. Basically trying to find the action which can bring maximum action value in each state then form a sequence by combining these actions.

Here is the exercise. In this virtual environment, we can observe three states: 0, 1, 2 and two actions: 0 and 1. The corresponding transition probability and reward table is as below. In this exercise, transition probability means the state have the probability of remaining at the same state. For example, if we are at state 0 and take action 0, we have 50% probability stay at the same state. In addition, (1, 0, 0): 0.7 means if we at state 1 and take action 0, we can have 70% probability return to state 0 ; (2, 1, 1): 0.5 means if we at state 2 and take action 1, we can have 50% probability return to state 1.

scenariostateactionnext statetransition probabilityreward
a110000.51
a120010.51
a210100.20
a220110.81
b111000.71
b121020.32
b211110.60
b221120.43
c112021.00
c212110.51
c222120.50

Diagram of state, action and transition

Since we have complete all the requisite, we can run the code and get the result.

Optimal Policy: 0→0→1
Value Function: [10.16927311, 10.20689125, 9.26018305]

entry.title: Weekly thought
entry.content:
This is kind of like a record of my postgraduate life. I feel it is necessary to take note of my transitions in recognition of my future and what I have done in a regular intervals (1-2 weeks). From now to August, I have to finish a couple of issues listed in the table below.

IssueContent
1. Paper submissionSubmit manuscript to PSEP. 100% the editor will mail it back and ask me to add more citations from C-RISE and PSEP. However, the submission should be accomplished before August.
2. TOEFL practiceThe result of reading test is horrible. 21 definitely not a satisfying mark for a Chinese who has shortcomings in oral English expression and writing. For listening test, I think I have to train how to concentrate on current audio and not to think useless things.
3. Find a universityPurdue, to me, is the optimal option. The most important thing is I can’t tell which kind of university be a safe base.

entry.title: Trip in Nanjing
entry.content:
What we did in the weekend?

Basically, we didn’t decide to take outdoor activities at daytime (even at nighttime) due to the extreme hot climate. Thus, we turned out vision to indoor activities. And, to me, Nanjing is a city which has enormous delicious food, so I took my friends to have those splendid dishes, mainly.

1. Noodle

Recently, Nanjing’s noodle is very popular not only for the astonishing appearance (a huge pot-stewed meat) but also for its complex and amazing taste. We went to a noodle shop near Nanjing University (actually, Southeast University and Nanjing University both at same street) which is famous due to the large meatball where a salted yolk hide in it.

You can see from the picture, this costed me 46 yuan, which is quite expensive. However, the taste matched its price. It is too delicious that I have difficulty finding proper words to precisely describe it.

2. Duck

There’s a saying that roasted duck in Nanjing is the origin of Beijing roasted duck. I am 100% willing to believe that be a truth. The reason is simple: I don’t like the roasted duck being too greasy, and Nanjing roasted duck just at the balance point of fatty and dry. The soul of duck is the sweet- and-salty sauce. Dipping a piece of duck in the sauce can improve the taste and make the duck meat becomes much more tender. Sadly, my friends seemed not a fan of it. They preferred steamed rice roll.

3. Dishes

We went to the top restaurant in Nanjing. Cuisines here are more sweeter than other places. The restaurant become crowded at 11 am which is truly scare to see.

This is my fav.

Who can image pig’s kidney can be a dish?

4. Gallery

Can’t understand the mind of artists.

entry.title: MASON'S RADIO#1
entry.content:
A shooting star in pop music industry

XG (acronym for Xtraordinary Girls) is a Japanese hip-hop & RnB girl group belongs to AVEX but based in South Korea, which has 7 members (the eldest is born in 2002 and the youngest born in 2005). Names sorted by age from oldest to youngest are as follow: Chisa, Hinata, Jurin, Harvey, Julia, Maya, Cocona. They made their debut on March 18, 2022, with the release of the digital single “Tippy Toes”.

XG (From left to right, they are Hinata, Jurin, Julia, Cocona, Maya, Chisa, Harvey)

Career (2022 - Present)

NameDateType
Tippy Toes2022.5.18Single
Mascara2022.6.29Single
Shooting Star2023.1.25Single
New DNA2023.6.30Mini album
Winter Without You2023.12.8Single
Woke Up2024.5.21Single

On March 18, 2022, XG made their debut with the release of their all English-language digital single “Tippy Toes”. On June 29, they released their second all English-language single titled “Mascara”, followed by their first television appearance on South Korean Mnet’s M Countdown performing the single. On November 17, XG released a rap cypher video entitled Galz Xypher (Galz Xypher#2). The video became viral on a TikTok through an XG fan account, gaining over 15 million views on the platform.

On January 25, 2023, XG released their third all English-language single, “Shooting Star” along with its music video and an additional track, “Left Right”. On March 6, XG entered the Media base US Radio Top 40, becoming the first female Japanese artist and the first Japanese group to do so. On June 30, XG released “Grl Gvng” accompanied by its music video, as a pre-release single preceding their debut EP New DNA. On August 4, XG released “TGIF” accompanied by its music video, as the second pre-release single to their debut EP New DNA. On August 23, XG released “New Dance” accompanied by its music video, as the third and final pre-release single to their debut EP New DNA. On September 27, XG’s debut EP New DNA was released alongside its lead single “Puppet Show“, accompanied by its music video. The EP was certified gold by the Recording Industry Association of Japan (RIAJ) for shipments of over 100,000 copies.

XG’s first surge in popularity was due to “Shooting Star” and “Left Right”. Even though they already posted 2 singles, their make-ups and concepts were similar to other KPOP groups. In other words, they had not discovered their own unique characteristics. However, in “Shooting Star” and “Left Right”, the music perfectly struck a chord with the audience’s hearts while embracing the millennial retro trend. To me, another reason that can’t ignore is there lyrics are English, which is more easier for audience to listen, remember and perform. Personally, I prefer left right to shooting star, the former one is a typical RnB which fits my bias really well.

A Cut of "Left Right"

The lyrics in “Shooting Star” vividly depicted their trainee experience: “Always lookin’ fresh, got that green in my diet”, “Everything I do, I do it A1, flawless” sang by Jurin; “Babe, if I give it my all, will it pay off? Workin’ overtime, no days off” by Chisa; “Now I’m goin’ up, headin’ to the stars. Wouldn’t trade it out for another life” by Julia. These lyrics also perfectly showcase the team’s efforts to achieve their dreams and their pride of being one of the team. In past 7 years, they devoted countless effort in practice, encountered with numerous psychological and life challenges, as well as a brutal elimination process. Now, these seven wolves can stand on the spotlighted stage and calmly yet proudly singing:“It’s a big move, every day’s like a dream, Makin’ big moves, yeah, we do it for our team, Ooh, ah, I’m lookin’ so lavish, Ooh, ah, yeah, I bet you can’t imagine.” What’s more, the dance performance can kill every KPOP girl group. Since I know a little about dance, I recommend this video for better understanding of their dance and why they outperform other peers.

“New DNA” was a bold and successful endeavor on the integration of concept and musicality together with visual performance. Before this, the continuity of the concept and the diversity of the music in this album were unimaginable. I am going to explain this point of view from the storyline and soundtracks.

Concept photo of Girl Gang

We have to start with the intro, “Hesonoo”. In Japanese, it means “umbilical cord”. It is also the slogan of the group, representing their their closely connected lives and emotions. Next up is the song “x-gene”, the two songs symbolize the awakening of extraterrestrial beings and the closely-knit team relationship; “Puppet Show” tells the story of them breaking free from their restraints; “Grl Gvng” is more like a superhero movie. This song intends to express their own incomparable strength and issue a severe warning to their enemies; “TGIF” and “New Dance” tells the story of the experiences and encounters of seven extraterrestrials after landing on Earth. Overall, it revolves around the theme of female self-awareness awakening, while blending in the typical hard-edged style of hip-hop. Truly, those girl groups, IVE, LE SSERAFIM, GIDLE are all playing such thing. However, it seems lack of credibility when those girls dressed in revealing outfits, with altered faces, performing lackluster dances, sing songs telling women not to care about others’ gazes, all I know is they are playing with females’ trust and trying to make genuine money by relying on these hollow elements. Their music production and choreography are outstanding as well. We can hear drill hi-hat clicking in the embryo, we can feel the drum shaking from the puppet world, we can dance to the bass blended in new dance. I deeply believe many people who were new to XG became fans of them through the “POP POP POP” section made by Harvey and I am one of them. New Dance gives me a totally different feel when comparing to other music in the album. I can find chill and relax in this song, I can rest my body but move my heartbeat with their dance steps. A cool and sweet soda definitely be much more popular than Jack Daniels in a hot summer.

The sickest shot I've ever seen in my life

“Woke Up” is A true revolution in the history of girl group. It is so hard to imagine that an entire rap song performed by a girl group and the visionary is so fierce, strong, and impressing. overlook the astonishing details and seamless scene transitions hidden in the MV but focus on what these girls doing because it is impossible for me to find and explain every one of them. We can ignore Jurin rapping in fire and the “Tokyo drift” bar, we can brush off Maya’s machinegun-like flow, we can pass over Harvey drop from outer space and reveal her true self in the midst of the flames, we can overlook the sick eye and catchy flow made by Hinata and Julia, we can set aside Chisa’s captivating melodic rap. But, we can not take no notice off what Cocona does, To me, that is the soul of this song. You can say she shaved her hair in music video is for attention and clout, you also can blame the producer for asking her give up her black hair and scoff at the announcement that she wanted to do it herself. Nevertheless, this has already become A FACT. SHE ACTUALLY DID THIS AND NOT JUST MAKE IT EXISTS IN TALKS. She has left many people shocked and in awe of what she has done.

After all, it is their 100% dedication to practice, musical diversity, and perfect teamwork that garnered them much attention and love around the globe. The XG MOVE, XG VOX or XG XYPHER all showcased their professionalism, hard work, and talent in performance, as well as their ease in mastering various genres. Tippy toes and Mascara both too Korean and can not help them establish a solid foothold in the music market which full of competence. Luckily, they find out the optimal method to express themselves and continuously striving forward on this path. Therefore, their success is well-deserved. But to me, I believe they still have not touched the ceiling. Such a valuable team deserves to enjoy larger stages, sharing their musical vision, stories and charm with a broader audience.

> Weekly music radio

The pre-song of XG’s second album “Something ain’t right” is going to online on July 26. I’ve heard the full song when writing this. I’d like make some descriptions for this song: It is not right but excellent. I admit that I view their works through “fan filter”, but try to listen and compare with other peers then judge whether I’m being fair if feeling curious or skeptical.

entry.title: Academic discussion#1
entry.content: I totally agree with Paul’s idea that government should not set automobile-free zone in city center , not only for the concern of businesses but also for the convenience of individuals. Since the center of a city is the core of local economy, politics and culture, it has to be capable of handling large quantity of people who come here for business efficiently. For Claire’s opinion, it is true that we have to work together to decrease the emit of carbon dioxide. However, automobile-free zone is not the only choice since we already found and invented the alternative of conventional petrol. What’s more, only pedestrian could turn city center becoming crowded and then increasing the risk of the occurrence of severe accidents. A street full of people can have negative influence on those who try to enjoy the shopping.
